concerns.text: FR-006 and SC-004 explicitly require a paired t-test (runtime) and McNemar's test (accuracy). The plan (Methodology Section 3) and tasks T025/T026 replace these with a Permutation Test and Linear Regression. There is no task to implement the specific statistical tests mandated by the spec (FR-006), creating a coverage gap where the spec's functional requirement is unimplemented.

FR-003 and SC-005 explicitly require testing the discrete threshold set {0.90, 0.95, 0.99}. Task T034 describes iterating through 'a range of high-confidence thresholds' without specifying these exact values. Without a task explicitly enforcing these three specific data points, the sensitivity analysis may fail to produce the required trade-off curve data points defined in the spec.

FR-007 and the Plan (Methodology Section 1) require generating two distinct datasets: a 'test set' and a 'warm-up set'. Task T005 only specifies generating a single file `data/derived/synthetic_queries.json`. There is no task to generate or separate the warm-up set, leaving the 'Warm-up Phase' described in the plan unimplementable.

FR-008 requires validating ground-truth source independence. Task T029b attempts to 'verify ground-truth source independence' in `code/analysis/metrics.py` (post-run). Independence is a property of the generator logic (FR-007) and should be validated at generation time (T029a) or via code inspection. There is no task to perform the actual validation of the generator's independence logic before execution, leaving FR-008 unmet.

Task T030 generates `data/derived/results.csv` but lacks a definition of what specific metrics go into the CSV versus the `statistics.json` (T031). This ambiguity risks data duplication or inconsistency, though the existence of the task covers the general requirement to generate results.
